verbGlossary.BLOCKS: Unresolved permit or open regulatory item prevents {target} element from being achieved (e.g., open permits block project completion milestone).
verbGlossary.CATEGORIZED AS: {source} event is formally classified within {target} analysis framework (e.g., regulatory hold is categorized as an excusable delay).
verbGlossary.CLOSES OUT: {source} certificate marks the final achievement of {target} element (e.g., certificate of occupancy closes out the substantial completion milestone).
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: {source} limits the scope or capacity of {target} element (e.g., labor compliance constrains crew size in labor loading).
verbGlossary.COORDINATED WITH: Inspection or regulatory scheduling must align with {target} element to avoid conflicts (e.g., inspection scheduling coordinated with look-ahead schedule).
verbGlossary.DRIVES: {source} processing duration determines the length of {target} element (e.g., plan review turnaround drives lag time between design and construction activities).
verbGlossary.ENABLES: {source} issuance or approval unlocks {target} element to proceed (e.g., building permit enables detail activities to start).
verbGlossary.ERODES: {source} processing time consumes available buffer in {target} element (e.g., long permit cycle times erode float on near-critical activities).
verbGlossary.FINALIZES: {source} certificate confirms {target} record is complete and locked (e.g., certificate of completion finalizes the as-built schedule).
verbGlossary.GATES: {source} or regulatory approval is a hard prerequisite that must be obtained before {target} element can be reached (e.g., building permit gates the construction-start milestone).
verbGlossary.GOVERNS: {source} plan has ongoing authority over how {target} element is executed (e.g., site safety plan governs daily work activities).
verbGlossary.HOLDS: {source} review or inspection pauses {target} element until clearance is granted (e.g., fire marshal review holds work activities pending approval).
verbGlossary.IMPOSES: {source} creates a formal constraint on {target} element (e.g., environmental permit imposes a seasonal work window as a schedule constraint).
verbGlossary.INFLUENCES: {source} metric indirectly affects {target} performance indicator (e.g., inspection pass rate influences schedule performance index through rework avoidance).
verbGlossary.JUSTIFIES: {source} hold provides documented basis for {target} claim (e.g., regulatory hold justifies a time extension request).
verbGlossary.LANDS ON: {source} activity falls on {target} analysis path due to its zero-float dependency (e.g., AHJ inspection lands on the critical path).
verbGlossary.RECORDED IN: {source} result is documented as part of the target schedule lifecycle event (e.g., special inspection outcome recorded in {target} update narrative).
verbGlossary.RELEASES: {source} document lifts a constraint previously imposed on {target} element (e.g., letter of compliance releases a regulatory hold constraint).
verbGlossary.REPORTED IN: {source} tracking status is communicated through the target schedule lifecycle document (e.g., permit status reported in {target} update).
verbGlossary.REQUIRED BEFORE: {source} or regulatory action must be obtained before {target} element can legally begin or be established (e.g., building permit required before baseline schedule is set).
verbGlossary.RESTRICTS: {source} condition limits the available working time in {target} element (e.g., environmental permit restricts work calendar to exclude nesting seasons).
verbGlossary.SCHEDULED AS: {source} process is formally represented as {target} element (e.g., permit application scheduled as a detail activity with defined duration).
verbGlossary.SEQUENCED INTO: {source} or review is inserted into {target} at the required point (e.g., trade permits sequenced into the look-ahead schedule before rough-in activities).
verbGlossary.SHIFTS: {source} review outcome moves {target} dates forward or backward (e.g., variance approval shifts calculated dates on design activities).
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} event initiates {target} analysis or recovery action (e.g., regulatory hold triggers a time impact analysis).
verbGlossary.VALIDATES: {source}testing confirms {target} element has been properly achieved (e.g., third-party testing validates a commissioning milestone).
